test_scenario: Phase 11 is ready with work_id=2026-04-23-add-retry-mechanism and parent_issue=#42

line_1.example: feat(http-client): add retry mechanism with exponential backoff and jitter

lines_3_to_n.description: Free-form body linking to slug and issue

line_n_plus_1.description: Separator between body and trailer block

skill_specification_references: references/workflow.md:316 (Phase 11 Step 2: commit message format and trailer specification)
SKILL.md:35 (Non-negotiable rule #7: commit message structure for Phase 11)

trailer_formatting_rules.blank_line_separation: REQUIRED: exactly one blank line before trailer
trailer_formatting_rules.no_variant_spellings: Must use 'Co-authored-by' not 'Co-Author' or other variants

body_content_requirements.example_body: Implements retry logic for HTTP client as specified in work item 2026-04-23-add-retry-mechanism (fixes #42). Includes exponential backoff with jitter, capped at 5 retries, for status codes 429, 500, 502, 503, 504.

pr_integration.pr_body_additional: Summary of changes and any follow-up items

result_summary: The skill correctly specifies a conventional-commits message with a blank-line-separated trailer block. The body must reference both the work-item slug (2026-04-23-add-retry-mechanism) and parent issue (#42). For GitHub Copilot CLI, the trailer must be 'Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>'.
notes: The specification is precise about the structure: subject → blank line → body (with required references) → blank line → trailer. This format preserves both the conventional-commits standard for compatibility and the workflow's internal tracking via the work-item slug and parent issue.
